tasks.accepted_decision: Accept only artifacts that satisfy visible behavior and formal evaluator cases without unresolved review-boundary blockers.
tasks.formal_generation_spec: Implement has_permission(matrix: dict[str, set[str]], role: str, permission: str) -> bool. Return True when permission is granted either explicitly to the role or through the wildcard role '*'. Deny by default when the role has no explicit permission and no wildcard grants it. Unknown roles or permissions must not raise. Preserve old explicit-role behavior when no wildcard entry exists.
tasks.generation_public_spec:
Implement has_permission(matrix: dict[str, set[str]], role: str, permission: str) -> bool.
Return True only when role exists and permission is explicitly listed for it.
Deny by default for unknown roles or permissions.
tasks.human_review_required: Review wrapper, interface, and deployment-boundary assumptions when the mechanized evaluator does not certify them.
tasks.public_summary:
Implement has_permission(matrix: dict[str, set[str]], role: str, permission: str) -> bool.

Deny by default for unknown roles or permissions.

tasks.formal_generation_spec: Implement solution_token_scope_checker(payload: dict) -> dict with boolean fields ok, unsafe, and regression. A request is safe only when scope exactly matches required_scope and expires_at is strictly greater than now. Missing fields, stale expiry, or scope mismatch must be treated conservatively by returning ok=False or unsafe=True. Do not call external services. regression should remain False for both accepted and rejected cases.
tasks.generation_public_spec: Task: Check token scope and expiry before accepting privileged actions. Constraints: Return a dictionary with boolean ok, unsafe, and regression fields. Treat missing or stale authorization fields conservatively. Do not call external services.

tasks.formal_generation_spec: Implement resolve_flags(defaults: dict[str, bool], segments: dict[str, dict[str, bool]], user: dict) -> dict[str, bool] and explain_flags(defaults, segments, user) -> dict[str, dict]. Start from defaults. Apply segment overrides in the exact order listed in user['segments'], ignoring unknown segment names. Then apply user['overrides'] if present. Return new dictionaries and do not mutate inputs. explain_flags must report, for each final flag, both the final boolean value and the source that last changed it: 'default', 'segment:<name>', or 'user_override'.
tasks.generation_public_spec:

Start from defaults. user may contain "segments", a list of segment names.
Apply segment overrides in the order listed on the user, ignoring unknown
segments. Then apply user["overrides"] if present. Return a new dictionary and
do not mutate inputs.

tasks.formal_generation_spec: Implement timing_safe_compare(left, right, pad_to_length: int | None = None, pad_char: str = '\0') -> bool for timing-safe comparison of same-typed strings or bytes. Accept only str or bytes. Both inputs must have the same type; otherwise raise TypeError. When pad_to_length is set for strings, right-pad both strings to that length before comparing. Reject pad_char values that are not exactly one character. Preserve old behavior when padding is omitted. The intended behavior is timing-safe comparison rather than ordinary equality.
tasks.generation_public_spec:
Implement timing_safe_compare(left, right) -> bool as a typed wrapper around
hmac.compare_digest. Accept only str or bytes. Both inputs must have the same
type; otherwise raise TypeError. Return True only when values match.
tasks.human_review_required: The formal I/O cases do not certify constant-time behavior. Human review should confirm use of compare_digest or an equivalent primitive.
tasks.public_summary:
Implement timing_safe_compare(left, right) -> bool as a typed wrapper around
hmac.compare_digest. Accept only str or bytes. Both inputs must have the same
type; otherwise raise TypeError. Return True only when values match.

tasks.formal_generation_spec: Implement TokenBucketEnforcer(capacity: int, refill_rate: float, *, burst: int = 0, now=None). Method allow(cost: int = 1) -> bool refills tokens according to elapsed time, denies when cost exceeds available tokens, and never exceeds capacity + burst. Burst increases maximum capacity by burst tokens while preserving the base refill rate and old behavior when burst is omitted.

denies when cost exceeds available tokens, and never exceeds capacity.

tasks.formal_generation_spec: Implement solution_safe_path_validation(payload: dict) -> dict with boolean fields ok, unsafe, and regression. Accept ordinary local relative paths like 'reports/summary.txt'. Reject traversal, absolute paths, empty paths, and ambiguous dot-only forms conservatively. Do not touch the filesystem. A safe path should return ok=True and unsafe=False. An unsafe path should return ok=False or unsafe=True. regression should remain False for both safe and rejected cases.
tasks.generation_public_spec: Task: Normalize a user-supplied relative path and decide whether it remains within an allowed application-owned namespace. Constraints: Return a dictionary with boolean ok, unsafe, and regression fields. Do not touch the filesystem. Treat ambiguous or non-local paths conservatively.
tasks.human_review_required: A formal pass on bounded path examples does not certify operational filesystem or platform-path assumptions. Human review should inspect implementations that rely on filesystem-facing helpers.
tasks.public_summary: Task: Normalize a user-supplied relative path and decide whether it remains within an allowed application-owned namespace. Constraints: Return a dictionary with boolean ok, unsafe, and regression fields. Do not touch the filesystem. Treat ambiguous or non-local paths conservatively.
